Meta.Title: Beijing 1-Day Classic Highlights (May)
Meta.Summary: One full day covering Beijing signature sights with a strong classic-highlights focus: Great Wall (Mutianyu recommended) plus Tiananmen Square and the Forbidden City. Built for May daylight and crowds, with clear ticketing and backup options if closures or timing changes occur.

Assumptions.Item: Day trip in May with long daylight and warm weather; start early to avoid crowds.
Base is central Beijing (Dongcheng/Xicheng/Chaoyang near subway access).
Forbidden City requires advance reservation and is typically closed on Mondays.

Block.title: Depart central Beijing for the Great Wall (Mutianyu)

Notes.Item: Best classic wall section for a 1-day plan: scenic, well-preserved, less chaotic than Badaling.
Transport options: private car/ride-hail, group shuttle, or chartered driver.
Bring water, light breakfast/snacks, sunscreen, and a light layer (mornings can be cool).

Block.title: Mutianyu Great Wall visit (3 hours on the wall)

Notes.Item: Take the cable car up (or chairlift) to save time; walk a classic out-and-back on the ramparts.
Optional: toboggan down for fun and speed (weather permitting).
Photo priorities: early morning watchtowers and ridge-line views.

Block.title: Quick lunch near Mutianyu or packed picnic

Notes.Item: Keep it efficient to protect afternoon palace time.
If traffic is heavy, shorten later stops and prioritize Forbidden City interior.

Block.title: Security and entry for Tiananmen Square area

Notes.Item: Expect airport-style security and possible ID/passport checks.
Do not carry prohibited items; leave extra batteries, drones, and large tools behind.
Keep this brief; the main goal is the Forbidden City.
Enter from the south (Meridian Gate) and follow the central axis: major halls and key courtyards.
Focus stops: Hall of Supreme Harmony area, Palace of Heavenly Purity zone, then exit north (Gate of Divine Might).
Last entry times can be early; verify on booking day and adjust the Great Wall duration if needed.

Block.title: Jingshan Park sunset viewpoint (optional but recommended)

Notes.Item: Short uphill walk; classic panoramic view over the Forbidden City (great in clear May evenings).

Block.title: Classic Beijing dinner (Peking duck or imperial-style cuisine)

Notes.Item: Book ahead if possible; ask for a smaller set if dining as two.
Post-dinner stroll option: nearby hutong lanes for atmosphere if you still have energy.

Variant.name: If Forbidden City is closed or sold out
Variant.Item: Swap afternoon to Temple of Heaven (Tiantan): focus on the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests and surrounding park paths.
Pair with a hutong neighborhood walk in the early evening.

Variant.name: If you want more Temple of Heaven without dropping the Great Wall
Variant.Item: Start earlier (05:00 departure), shorten wall time to about 2 hours, and visit Temple of Heaven before dinner.

Variant.name: If you prefer the closest Great Wall logistics
Variant.Item: Choose Badaling for fastest transport options, but expect heavier crowds; go as early as possible.

TicketsAndEntry.Item: Reserve Forbidden City in advance using your passport details; confirm open day and entry time window.
Great Wall entry and cable car/toboggan tickets can be purchased on-site or via your transport provider; bring cashless payment options.

WhatToBring.Item: Passport (or official ID), comfortable walking shoes, sun protection, water, light snacks, and a portable charger.

PacingNotes.Item: This is a long walking day; use cable car options at the wall to save your legs for the palace.
Prioritize the Great Wall and Forbidden City as the two core classics; everything else is adjustable.
